exercises.title: Standardize a feature
exercises.prompt: Implement z-score standardization (subtract mean, divide by std) and apply it to a feature. Explain why distance- and gradient-based models need it, and the critical rule about computing the statistics.
exercises.hint: z = (x − μ)/σ using TRAINING-set μ and σ, then apply the same to test data.

exercises.title: Target encoding and its leakage trap
exercises.prompt: Implement target encoding for a categorical feature (replace each category with the mean target for that category) and explain the data-leakage risk plus the standard mitigation.
exercises.hint: Naive target encoding uses each row's own target in its category mean → leakage. Fix with out-of-fold / smoothing.

exercises.title: TF-IDF from scratch
exercises.prompt: Build a TF-IDF vectorizer from scratch over a tiny corpus and explain why it beats raw word counts for text classification — specifically what the IDF term does to common vs rare words.
exercises.hint: TF = term freq in a doc; IDF = log(N / docs containing term). TF-IDF = TF × IDF.
